sector.name: Diagnostics
sector.description: Diagnostics chains scaling specialty and wellness testing

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹2,763 Cr (+12% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹752 Cr (+8% YoY)
FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 27% and PAT stood at ₹510 Cr (+4% YoY)
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹703 Cr (+17% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹187 Cr (+11% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹132 Cr, with PAT margin of 19%
Sample volume grew 13% in Q4FY26 and 10% in FY26

guidance.value: Specialized testing, Swasthfit, Sovaaka and M&A

highlights: FY26 growth was driven by sample volume growth and higher organized diagnostic preference
Company added 14 new labs and 1,100+ collection centers during FY26
Swasthfit contributed 27% of total revenue in FY26
Medllumina 2026 was launched for specialized scientific testing areas
Sovaaka was launched as an AI-powered premium wellness platform
Management will reinvest extra operating leverage into growth
Dr Lal Pathlabs Ltd.quote: "Dr. Lal is outperforming the sector on scale, balance sheet strength, scientific leadership, Swasthfit traction and specialized testing push."

fyPerformance: FY26 group revenue stood at ₹1,646 Cr (+24% YoY), while group EBITDA stood at ₹401 Cr (+32% YoY)
Group EBITDA margin stood at 24% and group PAT stood at ₹191 Cr (+31% YoY)
Q4FY26 group revenue stood at ₹425 Cr (+23% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹108 Cr (+71% YoY)
Organic FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,510 Cr (+14% YoY), ahead of 12-13% guidance
Organic PAT stood at ₹194 Cr (+33% YoY), with organic EBITDA margin of 26%

guidance.value: Selective productivity, specialty, tech and digital investments

highlights: Q4 organic revenue grew 15%, led by 9% patient volume growth and 5% realization improvement
Organic margins expanded around 140 bps to 26%
Group FY26 B2C revenue grew 19%, while B2B revenue grew 31%
TruHealth contributed 18% of group FY26 revenue and grew 27%
Company expanded presence to 750+ towns and cities
Core Diagnostics moved from negative EBITDA to high-single-digit EBITDA in Q4
Metropolis Healthcare Ltd.quote: "Metropolis is outperforming the sector on margin recovery, specialty mix, Core integration, B2C stickiness and organic growth execution."

fyPerformance: FY26 consolidated revenue stood at ₹829 Cr (+21% YoY), while standalone revenue stood at ₹774 Cr
FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹262 Cr (+38% YoY), while PAT stood at ₹163 Cr (+81% YoY)
Q4FY26 consolidated revenue stood at ₹224 Cr (+20% YoY), while standalone revenue stood at ₹210 Cr (+21% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹49 Cr (+128% YoY), with PAT margin of 22%
Thyrocare processed 210 Mn tests in FY26 and served 19.2 Mn patients

guidance.value: Franchise maturation, partnerships, insurance health checks and specialty testing

highlights: FY26 growth was driven by franchisee and partnership business
Pathology revenue grew 22% in FY26, while radiology declined 6% due to strategic consolidation
Active franchisee base reached 10,800
Jaanch grew 66% YoY in Q4FY26, though still around 2% of pathology revenue
Allergy testing was introduced using the Phadia platform with 250+ SKUs
Genomics foray started from May 2026 across NIPT, carrier screening and cancer predisposition panels
Thyrocare Technologies Ltd.quote: "Thyrocare is outperforming the sector on test volume scale, PAT growth, B2B franchise model, affordable positioning and specialty expansion."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹814 Cr (+20% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹337 Cr (+23% YoY)
FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 41%, while PAT stood at ₹173 Cr
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹219 Cr (+27% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹96 Cr (+39% YoY)
Q4FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 44% and PAT stood at ₹48 Cr (+38% YoY)
Q4FY26 test volume grew 19%, with radiology contributing 37% of revenue

guidance.value: Fully automated Panjagutta lab in Hyderabad

highlights: FY26 was a landmark year as revenue crossed ₹800 Cr
Vijaya delivered 17% revenue CAGR over 5 years without EBITDA margin dilution
Centers increased from 81 to 162 over 5 years
Footprint expanded from 2 states to 6 states
Growth was driven by both pathology and radiology
New hubs at Khammam and Nandyal achieved breakeven within 2 quarters
Vijaya Diagnostic Centre Ltd.quote: "Vijaya is outperforming the sector on B2C mix, integrated pathology + radiology model, EBITDA margin, wellness traction and new hub breakeven timelines."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹314 Cr (+23% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹99 Cr (+16% YoY)
FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 32%, while PAT stood at ₹31 Cr (+1% YoY)
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹82 Cr (+25% YoY), while EBITDA stood at ₹25 Cr (+26% YoY)
Q4FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 31%, while PAT stood at ₹6 Cr
FY26 tests performed stood at 8.16 Mn, with 6 tests per patient

guidance.value: One each in Bihar, Tripura and Jharkhand

highlights: FY26 growth was supported by network expansion, clinical capability and technology-led service improvement
Pathology contributed 49% of operational revenue and radiology contributed 44%
Company launched 2 hub centers, 11 spoke centers and 3 PPP centers during FY26
Mature centers continue to deliver 36-37% EBITDA margins
Genexus NGS platform enables oncology genomic results in 48-72 hours
MoU signed with IIT Kharagpur for AI-led Alzheimer detection using routine brain MRI
Suraksha Diagnostic Ltd.quote: "Suraksha is outperforming smaller regional peers on integrated radiology-pathology model, eastern India depth, genomics capability and hub-and-spoke expansion."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹773 Cr, while EBITDA stood at ₹215 Cr
FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 28% and PAT stood at ₹101 Cr
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹193 Cr, up 4% YoY and 7% QoQ
Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹56 Cr, with EBITDA margin of 29%
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹42 Cr, up 101%

guidance.value: Around ₹500 Cr across Rajasthan, CT / MRI, retail and backend
Expected to be maintained despite expansion costs

highlights: Krsnaa is building an integrated radiology + pathology platform through the PPP model
Tests processed stood at 59 Mn and patients served stood at 20 Mn
DSO improved to 139 days vs 155 days QoQ
Company operates 190+ CT / MRI centers and 147 pathology labs
Presence spans 18 states and UTs with 4,700+ patient collection centers
Retail currently contributes around 8% of revenue and is becoming a strategic cash flow driver
Krsnaa Diagnostics Ltd.quote: "Krsnaa is outperforming the sector on PPP scale, radiology leadership, long-term visibility, affordable pricing and integrated platform depth."

fyPerformance: FY26 consolidated revenue stood at ₹142 Cr, while consolidated PAT stood near ₹60 Cr
3B India molecular diagnostics sales stood at ₹86 Cr, up 10%
TRUPCR Europe sales stood at ₹21 Cr vs ₹14 Cr last year
TRUPCR Europe has delivered around 40% CAGR over the last 4 years
Coris BioConcept contributed ₹36 Cr sales and ₹9 Cr PAT for 7 months post acquisition

guidance.value: Expected to broadly grow in line with revenue
Submission process ongoing for selected AMR products

highlights: FY26 was supported by Indian molecular diagnostics, TRUPCR Europe and Coris BioConcept
3B India growth was below expectations due to high FY25 base from flu, dengue and chikungunya demand
Middle East geopolitical tension impacted some export markets
Management continues to focus on government labs, private labs and reagent rental programs
Coris acquisition has started contributing meaningfully to consolidated revenue and PAT
BSI has been appointed as notified body for IVDR transition
3B Blackbio DX Ltd.quote: "3B BlackBio is outperforming the sector on PAT margin profile, molecular diagnostics positioning, international expansion and regulatory approval optionality."
